title: Mask R-CNN
authors: Kaiming He, Georgia Gkioxari, Piotr Dollár, Ross Girshick
hook: Mask R-CNN lets computers color inside the lines for every single object.
promise: You will understand how Mask R-CNN detects objects and draws precise masks for each instance.
prerequisite_note: If you know what detecting objects in a photo means, you are ready to learn Mask R-CNN.

glossary.plain: Finding and outlining each individual object in an image, not just classifying or boxing them.
A rectangle drawn around an object to show where it is in an image.
A neural network that suggests likely locations for objects in an image.
A technique for accurately extracting small features from proposed regions without losing alignment.
A pixel-level label that shows exactly which pixels belong to an object.
An earlier model that finds and classifies objects, but only with bounding boxes.

cards.title: Draw the outline, not just the box
body: Learn how Mask R-CNN lets AI not only spot objects but also color them in.

cards.title: How can a computer see objects, not just shapes?
body: Imagine looking at a crowded photo and not just spotting people, but carefully tracing the edge of each one. This is harder than drawing boxes.
Early computer vision models could only draw rough rectangles around things. Mask R-CNN lets computers tell exactly which pixels belong to every object, no matter how tangled.

cards.title: From images to detailed outlines
body: First, the model finds promising areas in the image where objects might be hiding. Then, it zooms in on each region and figures out what is there.
Finally, instead of stopping at a box, Mask R-CNN draws a crisp mask that matches the object's true shape, even when objects overlap.
cards.caption: Mask R-CNN combines detection and fine-grained segmentation in one pipeline.

cards.title: Boxes or masks: what is the difference?
body: Slide to see how object detection changes when you add masks.
cards.offNote: Objects are found, but boundaries are rough and overlap is unclear.
cards.onNote: Each object gets a clean, pixel-perfect outline, even in crowded scenes.

cards.title: The simple branch that changes everything
body: Mask R-CNN keeps all the parts of Faster R-CNN but adds a parallel branch that predicts a mask for each object, not just a class and box.
The trick is to use a new layer, RoIAlign, which avoids losing pixel alignment, making segmentation masks much more accurate without slowing down training.

cards.title: How precise do you want your object outlines?
body: Move the slider to see what happens as mask accuracy improves.
cards.low: Objects are found, but fine details and overlaps are missed.
cards.mid: Masks are better, but edges can still be jagged or misaligned.
cards.high: Every object is traced almost exactly, even in complex scenes.

cards.title: What do you need for Mask R-CNN?
body: Pick the ingredients that make Mask R-CNN work.

choices.label: Segmentation mask branch
choices.note: Draws a mask for each object.

cards.title: How much better is Mask R-CNN?
body: Mask R-CNN topped all three COCO challenge tracks: detection, segmentation, and keypoints. It set a new standard for practical instance segmentation.
It is fast enough for real use and easy to adapt for other tasks like pose estimation, making it a foundation for modern vision models.
cards.caption: Mask R-CNN outperformed all previous single-model entries on major vision benchmarks.

cards.title: Why Mask R-CNN matters today
body: By simply adding a mask branch, Mask R-CNN made precise object segmentation practical and reliable for real-world images.
Its ideas now power many computer vision systems, from medical imaging to self-driving cars, wherever knowing the exact shape of objects is critical.

recap.label: Extract
recap.note: Pull features from the image with a CNN

recap.label: Propose
recap.note: Suggest regions likely to contain objects

recap.label: Classify
recap.note: Predict class and refine box for each region

recap.label: Segment
recap.note: Draw a mask for every object instance
